description:
You are helping a junior penetration tester who has just finished an automated network scan.  
All findings were automatically written into a SQLite database that is already present on the machine.

Location of the database file (already exists):
        /home/user/vuln_scans/scan_results.db

Inside that database there is exactly one table, called “vulnerabilities”, created like this:

        CREATE TABLE vulnerabilities(
                id        INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
                host      TEXT,
                port      INTEGER,
                service   TEXT,
                severity  TEXT,
                cvss      REAL
        );

The junior tester only needs a quick CSV list of the **HIGH-severity** findings, without the “severity” column itself, so that the list can be fed into another tool.

Your job:
1. From inside a bash shell, issue **one single terminal command** (no newline-chained or multi-line scripts; a single command invocation that can be copy-pasted and run as-is) that:
   • Connects to the SQLite database mentioned above.  
   • Selects every row whose “severity” column is exactly the string HIGH (case-sensitive match).  
   • Returns the columns in the following exact order and spelling: id,host,port,service,cvss (five columns total, no “severity” column).  
   • Writes the resulting rows (including a header) to the new file  
        /home/user/vuln_scans/high_vulns.csv  

2. Required CSV layout of /home/user/vuln_scans/high_vulns.csv (the automated grader will compare byte-for-byte):
   • First line is the header line:  
           id,host,port,service,cvss  
     (all lowercase, comma-separated, **no extra whitespace**, followed by a single UNIX newline “\n”).  
   • After the header, each result row must appear on its own line, comma-separated, with the same five fields in the same order, **sorted by the id column in ascending numeric order**.  
   • Every line (including the last data line) must end with a single UNIX newline character.  
   • Do not include blank lines or any other columns.  
   • Example of the exact final file layout (header plus two rows, each ending in “\n”):  
           id,host,port,service,cvss\n  
           1,192.168.1.10,22,ssh,9.0\n  
           3,192.168.1.11,445,smb,8.6\n  

Hints you may find useful (do **not** quote these literally in your command):
   • `sqlite3` supports the `-header` / `.headers on` and `-csv` switches for CSV output.  
   • You may pipe or redirect as needed, but everything still has to be inside one logical shell command (e.g., using shell redirection “>”).  
   • Make sure the target directory `/home/user/vuln_scans` already exists and is writable by the current unprivileged user.

Verification:
The automated test will:
   1. Open `/home/user/vuln_scans/high_vulns.csv`.  
   2. Confirm it is exactly three lines long with the precise contents shown in the example above (including final newline after the last row).  
   3. Confirm no extra files were created or modified.  

Only the CSV file content matters; console output is optional and will be ignored by the grader.
truth:
Initial state (before the agent starts):
------------------------------------------------
Directory tree:
/home/user/vuln_scans/            (mode 755, owned by user)
/home/user/vuln_scans/scan_results.db

The SQLite database /home/user/vuln_scans/scan_results.db has the single table “vulnerabilities” populated with exactly these four rows:

id | host         | port | service | severity | cvss
---+--------------+------+---------+----------+-----
1  | 192.168.1.10 | 22   | ssh     | HIGH     | 9.0
2  | 192.168.1.10 | 80   | http    | MEDIUM   | 6.5
3  | 192.168.1.11 | 445  | smb     | HIGH     | 8.6
4  | 192.168.1.12 | 21   | ftp     | LOW      | 3.7

Expected final state (after successful task completion):
--------------------------------------------------------
A new file is present:
/home/user/vuln_scans/high_vulns.csv   (mode 644, owned by user)

Exact byte-for-byte contents of /home/user/vuln_scans/high_vulns.csv:

id,host,port,service,cvss
1,192.168.1.10,22,ssh,9.0
3,192.168.1.11,445,smb,8.6

Note:
• Each of the three lines above ends with a single '\n' (LF, 0x0A).  
• There are no trailing spaces on any line.  
• No additional files should be created or altered in /home/user or its subdirectories.  
• The command may write to STDOUT or STDERR, but that output is not checked.

The grader will fail the task if:
   – The CSV file is missing, misnamed, or in the wrong directory.  
   – Column order, header spelling/case, or newline termination deviates from the specification.  
   – The row ordering is not ascending by id, or severity column is present.
name: task_000000_128540d4
